questions.question: The ancient Indian university of Nalanda was established during the reign of which Gupta emperor?
questions.explanation: Nalanda university was established during the reign of Kumaragupta I.

questions.question: The Sufi saint who founded the Chishti order in India was
questions.explanation: Khawaja Moinuddin Chishti is credited with founding the Chishti order in India.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in the year
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 by Allan Octavian Hume.

questions.question: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in the year
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920.

questions.question: The ancient Indian text that describes the social and economic conditions of the Mauryan period is
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra is an ancient Indian text that describes the social and economic conditions of the Mauryan period.

questions.question: The Mughal emperor who built the Taj Mahal was
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal was built by Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.question: The British East India Company was granted a royal charter by Queen Elizabeth I in the year
questions.explanation: The British East India Company was granted a royal charter by Queen Elizabeth I in 1600.

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in the year
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1942.

questions.question: The ancient Indian kingdom of Magadha was ruled by the
questions.explanation: The ancient Indian kingdom of Magadha was ruled by the Haryanka dynasty.

questions.question: The Vijayanagara empire was founded by
questions.explanation: The Vijayanagara empire was founded by Harihara and Bukka Raya.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through a rift valley?
questions.explanation: Narmada river flows through a rift valley.

questions.question: The easternmost point of India is
questions.explanation: Indira Point is the easternmost point of India.

questions.question: The largest coal reserve in India is found in
questions.explanation: Jharkhand has the largest coal reserve in India.

questions.question: The mountain range that separates the Indian subcontinent from the rest of Asia is
questions.explanation: The Himalayas separate the Indian subcontinent from the rest of Asia.

questions.question: The longest river in South India is
questions.explanation: The Godavari is the longest river in South India.

questions.question: The state with the longest coastline in India is
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline in India.

questions.question: The climate of India is influenced by
questions.explanation: The climate of India is influenced by the Himalayas, the Indian Ocean, and the Thar Desert.

questions.question: The Western Ghats are also known as
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats are also known as Sahyadri.

questions.question: The highest peak in the Eastern Ghats is
questions.explanation: The highest peak in the Eastern Ghats is Arma Konda.

questions.question: The major iron ore deposits in India are found in
questions.explanation: The major iron ore deposits in India are found in Jharkhand, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Karnataka, and Goa.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Indian Constitution?
options: Federal system with a weak center
Unitary system with a strong center
Parliamentary system with an elected head of state
Presidential system with a directly elected head of state
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution establishes a parliamentary system where the head of state (President) is indirectly elected.

questions.question: What is the primary role of the Panchayati Raj institutions in India?
options: To provide judicial services at the local level
To deliver healthcare services at the grassroot level
To facilitate local self-governance and development
To oversee law and order in rural areas
questions.explanation: Panchayati Raj institutions aim to promote local self-governance, enabling local populations to take decisions on matters affecting their daily lives.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression, subject to reasonable restrictions.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of writ issued by the Supreme Court or High Courts in India?
questions.explanation: Injunction is not a type of writ under Article 32 or Article 226 of the Indian Constitution; the other options are all types of writs.

questions.question: What is the term of the President of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India serves a term of five years from the date of entry into office.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution discusses the procedure for amendment?
questions.explanation: Article 368 of the Indian Constitution outlines the procedure for amendment of the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the 73rd and 74th Amendments to the Indian Constitution?
options: To strengthen the role of the Union in the federal structure
To decentralize power to local governments
To enhance the judicial review of legislation
To increase the number of seats in Parliament
questions.explanation: The 73rd and 74th Amendments aimed at giving constitutional status to the three-tier Panchayati Raj system and the municipalities, thereby decentralizing power to local governments.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Fundamental Right under the Indian Constitution?
options: Right to Education as a Fundamental Duty
Right to Property as a Constitutional Right
Right to Freedom of the Press
questions.explanation: The Right to Constitutional Remedies is a Fundamental Right under Article 32 of the Indian Constitution, which allows individuals to approach the Supreme Court for enforcement of their Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the independence of the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Article 124 of the Indian Constitution deals with the establishment and independence of the Supreme Court.

questions.question: What is the process called when the Parliament passes a law that takes away or abridges any of the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Judicial Review is the process by which the judiciary reviews and potentially strikes down laws passed by the legislature if they violate the Fundamental Rights.
